web.markdown:
![OpenAI - ChatGPT Plus](https://getsby.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/OpenAI-ChatGPT-Plus.webp)

Virtual Card to pay for OpenAI API and ChatGPT Plus
===================================================

###### [Order your Virtual Card today](https://getsby.com/en/my-account/)

![Author Image](https://getsby.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/Getsby-Blog-Team-Logo-e1731662729941.png)[Getsby Team](https://getsby.com/)
 | January 26, 2026

Virtual Mastercard Card for OpenAI API and ChatGPT Plus
-------------------------------------------------------

Open a paid plan for OpenAI API’s or ChatGPT Plus or Pro today using the [Virtual Green Card](https://getsby.com/en/virtual-mastercard/virtual-green-card/)
 from Getsby. The Green Card is a reloadable Debit Mastercard that can be linked to pay for your ChatGPT Plus subscription ($20/month) or OpenAI API usage (billed per token).

Unfortunately, not everybody has access to a credit card and PayPal is not accepted payment method, making our cards a great alternative. Green Cards can easily be added to your account without you worrying if the card will be rejected.

Reloadable Virtual Card
-----------------------

The Green Card stands out as the perfect payment method for securing your $20/month ChatGPT Plus or $200/month Pro subscription, while also serving developers efficiently when acquiring API tokens — thanks to its practical monthly spending limits. Stay in control by topping up your balance anytime with a credit card or instant bank transfer.

For security reasons, please note that you can freeze/unfreeze your card at any time. This gives you complete control to prevent unwanted charges or hidden fees. As a bonus for using the Green Card, we plant a tree 🌳 for every € 200 you spend with your card.

###### [Get your Virtual Green Card](https://getsby.com/en/my-account/)

![Virtual Green Card](https://getsby.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/12/Virtual-Green-Card_Tekengebied-1-e1652647275960.png)

What is ChatGPT?
----------------

OpenAI and ChatGPT have been booming ever since the official launch on November 30th 2022. People have been exploring the possibilities, but what is it exactly? ChatGPT is an AI Chatbot initially developed by [Open AI](https://openai.com/)
 to be used for customer service. The chatbot is trained to respond like support teams would on customer questions. However, Chat GPT is also used for other solutions, like writing code, blog posts, stories and translating.

Is ChatGPT free?
----------------

The simple answer is, yes ChatGPT 4o mini is free. It’s free for personal use and commercial use. Seeing that the tool is a freemium, OpenAI’s strategy is most likely to have as many people use it to obtain more data to fine-tune the model and service.

### ChatGPT Plus

OpenAI has a $20/month paid version, called ChatGPT Plus. Although the functionalities for the Plus plan are similar to the standard free plan, it does come with benefits. You get early access to the latest features, faster response times and you always have access to the chatbot whenever there is peak usage.

### ChatGPT Pro

Besides the Plus plan, OpenAI introduced ChatGPT Pro in December 2024. This subscription is a $200 monthly plan that is more advanced that gives you the highest level of access to the best of OpenAI’s models and tools. This includes unlimited access to GPT-4o and advanced speech, but also the use of to in-depth research tools, the o1 pro mode and the ability to create videos using Sora.

### OpenAI API

Their flagship product is a Pay as You go plan for OpenAI’s API – GPT-4o/GPT-4o mini/GPT-4.1 nano. You simply select a language model that suits your (developer) needs, and pay per 1M tokens used. You can choose between, but are not limited to, GPT-4o/GPT-4o mini/GPT-4.1 nano – which is the most powerful and similar to ChatGPT.

**Note:** Prices for monthly subscriptions in the EU are: GPT Plus – €23 and GPT Pro – €229.

What payment methods are accepted for OpenAi and ChatGPT Plus?
--------------------------------------------------------------

After creating an OpenAI account, users can select “[Payment methods](https://platform.openai.com/account/billing/payment-methods)
” under the Billing tab to add a payment card. Any of the following cards are accepted:

*   Mastercard
*   Visa
*   American Express
*   Discover
*   Diners Club

The same methods are accepted to pay for ChatGPT Plus and Pro.

Are there alternative payment methods available for OpenAI subscriptions?
-------------------------------------------------------------------------

The most common alternative payments for OpenAI subscriptions are PayPal and Bank Transfers. We briefly describe them below.

### Is PayPal accepted to pay for ChatGPT?

Unfortunately, you cannot use PayPal to pay for you subscription. OpenAI does not mention if they have any intention of adding PayPal to the payment methods.

### Can I upgrade to ChatGPT Pro or Plus via Bank Transfer?

Similar to PayPal, you cannot pay for your OpenAI subscription via Bank Transfer. Alternatively, it is possible to link a reloadable virtual card to your account, and top-up the card via Bank Transfers.

How to pay for ChatGPT without a credit card?
---------------------------------------------

We understand that not everybody has access to a credit card and PayPal is not an option. Others may not want to expose their real credit card details in a BETA system due to a possible data breach. In all of these cases our Virtual Cards are the way to go. They do not require a credit check and shield your real card details.

###### [Order your Virtual Card today](https://getsby.com/en/my-account/)

Discover the Power of Getsby Virtual Cards for OpenAI
-----------------------------------------------------

Unlock a smarter, safer way to pay for OpenAI services—whether it’s Chat GPT Pro or the API—with a Getsby Virtual Card. Here’s why it’s the perfect choice for your business or personal use:

*   **Top-Notch Security:** Keep your real credit card details safe. A Getsby Virtual Card acts as a shield, reducing the risk of data breaches or unauthorized charges, so you can focus on what matters.
*   **Effortless Accessibility:** No credit or debit card? No problem. Getsby enables payments for users in regions where OpenAI may decline local cards, making it ideal for non-US users or anyone without traditional payment options.
*   **Smart Spending Control:** Take charge of your budget. Reload your card to cover Chat GPT subscription or API token-based billing, and freeze or unfreeze it anytime to stay in control of your spending.
*   **Versatile Usage:** Beyond OpenAI, your Getsby Virtual Card works across platforms like [Reddit Premium](https://www.reddit.com/premium)
     or [Patreon](https://getsby.com/en/creator-platform/subscribe-to-patreon/)
    , offering unmatched flexibility for all your digital subscriptions.
*   **Enhanced Privacy:** Minimize sharing sensitive financial details with third-party services like OpenAI, keeping your information secure and your transactions discreet.

**Good to Know:** A 2% FX fee applies for non-Eurozone transactions, so ensure sufficient funds to cover fees. Calculate the correct value for your one time use card ([link](https://www.mastercard.us/en-us/personal/get-support/convert-currency.html)
) based on the current Mastercard exchange rates for your payments, including FX costs.

How to use your Getsby Virtual Card?
------------------------------------

Here is a quick step-by-step guide to setup your Getsby prepaid Mastercard for ChatGPT Plus.

1.  Create account on Getsby.com.
2.  Select the Green Card with the preferred balance and to the checkout.
3.  You will receive an access code for your virtual card by email within 2 minutes after your payment has been received.
4.  After activation, you have access to your 16-digit card number to subscribe to OpenAI.
5.  Log into your OpenAI account, go to “Payment Methods” under Billing, and enter the card details.
6.  Confirm the payment for Chat GPT Pro or Plus.

Using a virtual card, ensures your real [credit card number](https://getsby.com/en/debit-and-credit-card-expiry-dates-explained/)
 is secure. You can also setup the card in your OpenAI account to pay for your monthly invoices.

[![Pay for your subscription](https://getsby.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/12/CTA-virtual-prepaid-card-1.png)](https://getsby.com/en/my-account/)

Troubleshooting Payment Issues
------------------------------

In some occasion transactions with your virtual card can be declined.

There are a few common reasons for a card decline:

*   Insufficient funds in the account
*   The billing address does not match the card information or address on file
*   You’re entering incorrect card information

You will be able to retry up to three times before your card is blocked.

Final Thoughts
--------------

OpenAI mainly uses credit and debit cards for payments. This can be difficult for people that have no access to a Visa or Mastercard. While this way might help stop fraud and fits how the company works in the U.S., it leaves out many users worldwide who need easier ways to pay.

For these users, Getsby Virtual Cards offer a good solution. They let people sign up for paid services or use APIs, even in places where local cards often don’t work or alternative payments options are not available.

Secure Free Trials with One-Time Virtual Card
=============================================

*   [Case Studies](https://buvei.com/blog/category/case-studies/)

![](https://wordpress.buvei.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/Secure-Free-Trials-with-One-Time-Virtual-Card-1248x702.png)

2 min read

[](https://twitter.com/share?t=Secure%20Free%20Trials%20with%20One-Time%20Virtual%20Card&url=https://buvei.com/blog/secure-free-trials-with-one-time-virtual-card/ "Share in Twitter")
[](https://www.facebook.com/sharer.php?text=Secure%20Free%20Trials%20with%20One-Time%20Virtual%20Card&u=https://buvei.com/blog/secure-free-trials-with-one-time-virtual-card/ "Share on Facebook")
[](https://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://buvei.com/blog/secure-free-trials-with-one-time-virtual-card/ "Share in Linkedin")
[](https://buvei.com/blog/secure-free-trials-with-one-time-virtual-card/ "Copy link")

Free trials are a great way to test software, SaaS tools, or subscription services. However, most platforms still require a valid payment method, leaving your personal card exposed to potential charges once the trial ends.

**One-time virtual cards** offer a secure alternative, letting you complete trials without risking your main account. In this guide, we’ll explain why one-time cards are useful, how they work, and how to create them using Buvei.

![](https://wordpress.buvei.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/Secure-Free-Trials-with-One-Time-Virtual-Card.png)

Why Free Trials Still Require Payment Details
---------------------------------------------

Many online services require payment upfront to prevent abuse and ensure seamless service activation.

### **Key Points**

*   Platforms verify users with a valid card
    
*   Auto-renewal triggers charges after the trial period
    
*   Using a personal card can result in unexpected fees
    

This is where one-time virtual cards become a safer solution.

[![](https://wordpress.buvei.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/buvei.png)](https://buvei.com/?s=blog)

Risks of Using Personal Cards for Trials
----------------------------------------

Using your main card for free trials carries several risks:

*   **Unintended Charges** – Automatic renewals can occur without notice
    
*   **Data Exposure** – Your card details are stored on multiple platforms
    
*   **Fraud Vulnerability** – If a service is compromised, your personal card may be at risk
    

How One-Time Virtual Cards Work
-------------------------------

One-time virtual cards are temporary cards generated specifically for a single payment or transaction.

### **Benefits**

✅ Card expires automatically after use  
✅ Isolates your primary bank account  
✅ Prevents unexpected charges after trial ends  
✅ Works on international platforms

These cards are ideal for free trials, short-term subscriptions, and one-off online purchases.

Managing Trial Renewals Safely
------------------------------

Even with one-time cards, it’s important to monitor trial subscriptions:

*   **Track Expiration** – Note when trials end to avoid automatic renewals
    
*   **Freeze or Delete Cards** – Ensure no lingering active cards
    
*   **Monitor Transactions** – Check the Buvei dashboard for any unexpected charges

How to Pay OpenAI with Virtual Cards
====================================

*   [Case Studies](https://buvei.com/blog/category/case-studies/)

![](https://wordpress.buvei.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/How-to-Pay-OpenAI-with-Virtual-Cards-1248x702.png)

3 min read

[](https://twitter.com/share?t=How%20to%20Pay%20OpenAI%20with%20Virtual%20Cards&url=https://buvei.com/blog/how-to-pay-openai-with-virtual-cards/ "Share in Twitter")
[](https://www.facebook.com/sharer.php?text=How%20to%20Pay%20OpenAI%20with%20Virtual%20Cards&u=https://buvei.com/blog/how-to-pay-openai-with-virtual-cards/ "Share on Facebook")
[](https://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://buvei.com/blog/how-to-pay-openai-with-virtual-cards/ "Share in Linkedin")
[](https://buvei.com/blog/how-to-pay-openai-with-virtual-cards/ "Copy link")

As more businesses and developers rely on [OpenAI tools](https://buvei.com/blog/how-to-choose-virtual-cards-for-ai-tools/)
, stable subscription payments have become increasingly important. Whether you’re using API services or ChatGPT-based plans, billing interruptions can disrupt workflows.

Understanding how virtual cards for OpenAI billing work can help prevent payment failures and subscription pauses.

This guide explains how OpenAI billing functions, why some cards are declined, and how to set up a compatible virtual card step by step.

![](https://wordpress.buvei.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/How-to-Pay-OpenAI-with-Virtual-Cards-1.png)

How OpenAI Subscription Billing Works
-------------------------------------

OpenAI typically processes payments through standard card networks such as Visa and Mastercard. Billing may occur in two common ways:

*   Monthly recurring subscriptions
    
*   Usage-based API billing
    

Payments are automatically charged to the card on file. If authorization fails, access to services may be restricted until the issue is resolved.

Because billing systems rely on automated authorization, payment stability is critical.

Why Some Cards Get Declined by OpenAI
-------------------------------------

Card declines can occur for several reasons:

*   Insufficient balance
    
*   Bank-side fraud controls
    
*   Cross-border transaction restrictions
    
*   Region mismatch
    
*   Expired or inactive cards
    
*   Prepaid card limitations
    

In some cases, traditional bank cards may block international SaaS transactions.

Using a properly configured virtual card can reduce these risks.

Requirements for [OpenAI-Compatible Virtual Cards](https://buvei.com/blog/virtual-cards-that-work-with-openai-billing-avoid-payment-declines/)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A virtual card that works with OpenAI billing should support:

*   International online payments
    
*   Visa or Mastercard processing
    
*   Recurring transaction authorization
    
*   Sufficient available balance
    
*   Transparent transaction tracking
    

Stable authorization behavior is often more important than the card type itself.

Common OpenAI Payment Errors and How to Fix Them
------------------------------------------------

Here are several frequent issues and potential solutions.

### Error: Card Declined

*   Confirm sufficient balance
    
*   Check spending limits
    
*   Verify card is enabled for online transactions
    

### Error: Payment Authorization Failed

*   Ensure recurring billing is supported
    
*   Confirm BIN region compatibility
    
*   Avoid frequent card switching
    

### Error: Subscription Paused

*   Add backup payment method
    
*   Update card details
    
*   Confirm no transaction blocks
    

Maintaining consistent billing behavior helps prevent repeated authorization failures.

[![](https://wordpress.buvei.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/buvei-4.png)](https://buvei.com/?s=blog)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Step-by-Step: Paying OpenAI with a Virtual Card
-----------------------------------------------

Follow this structured process.

### Step 1: Choose a Virtual Card Platform

Look for a provider that offers:

*   Global Visa/Mastercard coverage
    
*   Recurring billing support
    
*   Clear backend transaction logs
    
*   Flexible spending limits
    
*   Instant card issuance
    

### Step 2: Register a Buvei Account

To create a compatible virtual card:

1.  Visit **[https://buvei.com](https://buvei.com/)
    **
    
2.  Register for a free account
    
3.  Verify your email
    
4.  Log into the dashboard
    

You will gain access to Wallet and Card management tools.

![](https://wordpress.buvei.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/%E5%9B%BE%E7%89%871-1.jpg)

### Step 3: Fund Your Account with USDT

Inside the dashboard:

1.  Go to the **Wallet** section
    
2.  Select **USDT (TRC20 or ERC20)**
    
3.  Copy your unique deposit address
    
4.  Transfer funds from your crypto wallet
    
5.  Wait for confirmation
    

After confirmation, your balance will be available for card creation.

![](https://wordpress.buvei.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/%E5%9B%BE%E7%89%872-1.jpg)

### Step 4: Create a Virtual Card

Now generate your payment card:

Navigate to the **Cards** tab

Select preferred BIN region

Choose card type

![](https://wordpress.buvei.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/9ec5e1010a8babcd15a6c6445baeff55.png)

Set spending limit

Click **Issue Card**

![](https://wordpress.buvei.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/%E5%9B%BE%E7%89%874-1.jpg)

After creation, visit **My Cards** to review:

*   Card number
    
*   Expiration date
    
*   CVV
    

![](https://wordpress.buvei.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/%E5%9B%BE%E7%89%875-1.jpg)

*   Transaction history
    

![](https://wordpress.buvei.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/%E5%9B%BE%E7%89%876-1.jpg)

### Step 5: Add the Card to OpenAI Billing

Inside your OpenAI account:

1.  Go to Billing Settings
    
2.  Select Add Payment Method
    
3.  Enter your virtual card details
    
4.  Save and confirm
    

Your subscription will now charge the virtual card automatically.

Managing OpenAI Subscription Stability
--------------------------------------

To reduce interruptions:

*   Keep sufficient balance
    
*   Avoid frequent payment method changes
    
*   Set appropriate spending limits
    
*   Monitor transaction history
    
*   Maintain a backup card if necessary
    

Using structured card management improves billing consistency.

![](https://wordpress.buvei.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/How-to-Pay-OpenAI-with-Virtual-Cards.png)

Conclusion
----------

Virtual cards for OpenAI billing provide a practical solution for users who experience card declines or need better subscription control.

By using a USDT-funded virtual card with recurring billing support and transparent tracking, users can:

*   Reduce payment failures
    
*   Maintain uninterrupted access
    
*   Manage SaaS expenses more effectively
    

Platforms like Buvei offer multi-card management and instant issuance, supporting structured and reliable OpenAI billing workflows.

The “free API key / trial credit” hook that many tutorials mention is often outdated. Many newer users report they never received any credits.

That means in practice, to use the API (even for small tests or proof-of-concepts) you normally need to add a valid payment method.  
Using the API is billed per use — there’s no “always-free forever” plan (at least not officially).

If I were building a small test project today:  
I’d prepare to spend a small amount on prepaid credits (just enough to cover a few dozen or hundred runs).  
I’d start with a simple, small model + minimal token usage + good prompt engineering so I only pay for what’s truly needed.  
I’d track usage carefully — even small experiments add up if you’re sloppy with prompts or output size.

However, there are several legitimate programs and alternatives that let you use OpenAI (or other LLMs) for free or at very low cost.

Here are some ways to Get Free or Subsidized Access to OpenAI API:

1.  Data Sharing Program (Up to ~11M free tokens/day)  
    OpenAI offers a Data Sharing Program where organizations can receive significant free usage in exchange for letting OpenAI use their prompts + completions for model improvement.

Key details:

*   Up to 11 million free tokens/day (across GPT-4o, GPT-4o mini, etc.)
*   Must explicitly opt-in through an application
*   Your account must have a positive balance (even a few dollars)
*   Not automatically granted — this is a formal program, not the toggle in account settings

2.  Researcher Access Program (Up to $1,000 in API credits)  
    OpenAI supports researchers working on:

*   Responsible AI
*   Societal impacts
*   Safety, ethics, or governance

Eligibility:  
Must be affiliated with a university, research lab, or nonprofit

3.  Startup Programs (VC-backed startups)

    OpenAI for Startups  
    Available to portfolio companies of VCs like:

*   Sequoia
*   Thrive Capital
*   a16z

*   and others  
    Some partners offer free OpenAI credits as part of their ecosystem benefits  
    Good option for early-stage founders with VC support.

*   Set strict max\_tokens and shorten outputs during testing
*   Cache repeated system prompts and responses
*   Stream responses to reduce unnecessary tokens
*   Prototype first using free models from OpenRouter or Gemini

**As of late 2025**:  
• OpenAI no longer hands out automatic free credits just for creating a new API account. You normally have to add a payment method and prepay a small amount before you can call the API. ￼  
• The "free tier" wording you see in docs usually refers to promo / grant credits if you happen to have them, not a standing free plan for everyone. ￼

So if you are just a regular indie dev spinning up a project, assume you will need to drop a few bucks to unlock the API.

Also worth noting: ChatGPT Plus and API billing are separate. Paying for ChatGPT does not give you API credits.

### Legit ways to get actual free credits

These all require fitting certain criteria, but they are real:

a) R**esearcher Access Program (academia / nonprofits)**

If you have an active affiliation with a university, research org, or qualifying nonprofit, you can apply for the OpenAI Researcher Access Program, which gives up to $1,000 in API credits for approved projects. ￼

Good fit if your "test project" can be framed as research on AI safety, societal impact, etc.

b) **OpenAI for Startups (VC-backed startups)**

If you are part of a startup backed by certain VC partners, OpenAI for Startups can provide API credits as part of their program. They ask for a referral code from your VC plus a short description of what you are building. ￼

So this is more for funded teams, not a solo side project, but good to know.

c) **Open source projects - Codex open source fund**

OpenAI runs a Codex Open Source Fund where open source projects can receive up to $25,000 in API credits. ￼

If your test project can be truly open source and interesting enough, this might be an option.

d) **Microsoft for Startups Founders Hub (Azure OpenAI)**

If you are ok using Azure OpenAI instead of direct OpenAI API, the Microsoft for Startups Founders Hub has a tier that includes around $2,500 in OpenAI credits as part of their package. ￼

That is arguably the most realistic "big free credit" route for a scrappy builder who can describe a startup idea.

e) **Event / hackathon credits**

OpenAI sometimes gives out promo credits via partners, hackathons, or events tied to your org ID. ￼

That is more luck / timing based, but if you join official hackathons you can occasionally get a chunk of usage for free.

### If you do pay a little: how to keep it dirt cheap

Assuming you toss in, say, $5–10:

a) **Use the mini models**

Mini models are much cheaper per token than the big flagship ones. Pricing for smaller models (gpt-4o-mini, GPT-5 mini, etc.) is in the ballpark of a tiny fraction of a dollar per million tokens for input, and still low for output, making them several times cheaper than flagship models. ￼

For a small POC, 5–10 dollars on a mini model can go a surprisingly long way.

b) **Keep prompts and responses small**

**A few practical tricks**:  
• Trim context: do not send full documents every call, send IDs or summaries.  
• Cap max\_tokens on responses so the model cannot ramble.  
• Use short system messages and reuse them from your app instead of resending giant prompt templates every time.

c) **Design prompts in ChatGPT first**  
• Iterate on your prompts in the ChatGPT UI until they do what you want.  
• Once the behavior looks good, port that prompt into your API code.  
• This avoids burning API tokens on every "tweak one word, try again" experiment.

d) **Be careful with tools like Web Search**

Tools like web search or very heavy models can be much more expensive per call than plain text completion. If your POC does not need search or vision, keep it simple at first. ￼

e) **Use usage caps and alerts**

**In the OpenAI platform you can**:  
• Set hard and soft usage limits.  
• Monitor per-project usage to see which feature is burning tokens. ￼

If you are worried about surprise bills, this is your safety net.

### "Workarounds" to reduce spend even more

**Nothing shady, just good habits**:  
• Prototype logic with open source models locally (Ollama etc.) where possible, then only switch to OpenAI API once the flow feels right.  
• Add a "model" flag in your app config so you can do:  
• dev: super cheap mini model  
• staging/prod: nicer, larger model when needed  
• Batch offline stuff (like generating a bunch of test data) using the Batch API, which can be discounted compared to normal calls.

\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\
\

\
\]\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\
\
[<- Articles](https://www.respan.ai/articles)\
\
OpenAI API Credits: Trial Bonuses, Auto-Recharge, and the Discounts That Cut Your Bill 75%\
==========================================================================================\
\
Prepaid billing, auto-recharge, free credits, and the two discounts that cut your bill by 75 percent.\
\
![OpenAI API Trial Credits: How to Get + Stretch Them (2026)](https://www.respan.ai/articles/openai-api-credits/og)\
\
OpenAI API credits are the prepaid balance that funds every call you make to the OpenAI API. Unlike the consumer ChatGPT subscription, the API is pay-as-you-go: you load credits into your developer account, every request burns a few cents off the balance, and when it hits zero your calls start returning 429 errors with an `insufficient_quota` code.\
\
If you are running production traffic on GPT-5.5 or GPT-5.4, the difference between a naive setup and one that uses prompt caching plus the batch API is roughly 4x on your monthly bill. That is the difference between $4,000 and $1,000 for the same workload. This guide walks through how credits actually work, how to get them, and the two levers that matter most for cost.\
\
For the broader cost-control story (multi-provider fallback, per-feature budgets, usage attribution), see our [LLM Gateway pillar](https://www.respan.ai/ai-gateway)\
.\
\
TL;DR\
-----\
\
*   OpenAI billing is prepaid. You load credits, the API spends them, you set auto-recharge if you want to avoid running dry.\
*   New accounts created after mid-2025 receive zero automatic free credits. The old $5 trial is gone for most regions; promotional credits exist but are not automatic.\
*   Two discounts stack and matter: prompt caching (up to 90 percent off cached input tokens) and the batch API (50 percent off across the board with a 24-hour SLA).\
*   Combined, cached batch input on GPT-5.4 costs about $0.625 per million tokens versus a list price of $2.50. That is roughly 75 percent off.\
*   The fastest way to drain credits is a runaway agent in a retry loop. Set a hard monthly budget cap in the platform settings on day one.\
\
How OpenAI API billing works in 2026\
------------------------------------\
\
OpenAI runs prepaid billing. There is no monthly invoice and no overdraft. You buy credits up front, your usage burns them down, and when the balance hits zero the API stops accepting requests.\
\
You have two top-up modes:\
\
1.  **Manual prepaid.** You click "Add to credit balance" and load a fixed amount ($10 minimum, $1,000 maximum per top-up depending on your tier). Credits expire 12 months after purchase if unused.\
2.  **Auto-recharge.** You set a threshold ($X remaining) and a top-up amount ($Y added). Whenever your balance dips below the threshold, OpenAI charges your card and adds the credit. This is the only safe option for production traffic.\
\
The credit balance is shared across every model, every endpoint, every team member in the organization. There is no per-project sub-balance from OpenAI directly; if you need that, you handle it in a gateway or in your own accounting layer.\
\
Note: a "credit" is just a dollar (or your local currency equivalent if you are billed outside USD). There is no virtual unit. $100 in credits equals $100 of API usage at whatever per-token rates apply to the models you call.\
\
Free credits for new accounts\
-----------------------------\
\
This is the section that gets out of date fastest, so be aware: as of May 2026, OpenAI does not automatically grant free credits to new developer accounts. The historical $5 trial credit was phased out for most new signups in mid-2025.\
\
What still exists:\
\
*   **Researcher Access Program.** Academics can apply for credit grants. Approvals take a few weeks and are not guaranteed.\
*   **Promotional partnerships.** Startup programs (YC, Microsoft for Startups, accelerators) occasionally bundle OpenAI credits. Check your accelerator's perks dashboard.\
*   **Regional promotions.** OpenAI runs occasional new-account credits in specific markets. They are not announced in advance.\
\
Bottom line: budget for paying from day one. Do not architect your project around the assumption of free credits. Always check the live pricing page at the time of signup for the current free-credit status, because this changes.\
\
Applying promo and partner credits\
----------------------------------\
\
If you do receive credit codes (from a hackathon, a startup program, a research grant), you apply them in the OpenAI platform billing UI under "Credit grants" or "Apply code". A few rules:\
\
*   Promo credits are spent **before** prepaid credits. The platform draws them down first automatically.\
*   They have an expiration date attached to the grant. You see it in the billing UI; it is usually 3 to 12 months.\
*   They are organization-scoped. Apply them on the org that will actually do the spending.\
*   They are not transferable between organizations. Pick the right org first.\
\
If your grant has an expiration in two weeks and you have not used it, that is a "use it or lose it" moment. Either run your eval suites against the latest models or burn it on a one-time bulk classification job.\
\
Monitoring usage so you don't get surprised\
-------------------------------------------\
\
There are three layers of monitoring, and you should run all three:\
\
1.  **OpenAI Usage dashboard.** Built-in, updates with about an hour lag, shows tokens and cost per model. Good for sanity checks, bad for real-time alerting.\
2.  **Webhook + email alerts.** Set a "soft" usage limit (a dollar threshold that triggers an email) and a "hard" monthly budget cap (which stops API calls). These live in the limits settings. Set both.\
3.  **Per-call attribution.** The Usage dashboard tells you "you spent $4,000 on GPT-5.5 this month." It does not tell you which feature, user, or prompt drove that. You need either a gateway or your own logging layer.\
\
For attribution, a gateway is the natural choke point. See [What is an LLM Gateway](https://www.respan.ai/articles/what-is-an-llm-gateway)\
 and [Best LLM Gateways in 2026](https://www.respan.ai/articles/best-llm-gateways)\
 for the framing. The short version: every request flows through the gateway, the gateway tags it with user, feature, environment, and writes the cost row to your logs. You can answer "which user is the top spender" in a SQL query instead of a multi-week guessing game.\
\
Stretching credits: prompt caching\
----------------------------------\
\
Prompt caching is the single biggest lever in 2026 for cutting OpenAI bills. The mechanic: when you send a request, OpenAI hashes the prefix of your input. If the same prefix was seen recently (within roughly 5 to 10 minutes of idle, longer with steady traffic), OpenAI serves those tokens from cache at a steep discount instead of recomputing.\
\
The discount is large. On GPT-5.4 standard pricing, input tokens drop from $2.50 per million to $0.25 per million when cached, which is 90 percent off. On GPT-5.5, the absolute discount per token is bigger because the list price is higher.\
\
Caching is automatic. You do not call a "cache" endpoint. To benefit, you structure your prompts so the long, stable part comes first and the variable user input comes last:\
\
    from openai import OpenAI\
     \
    client = OpenAI()\
     \
    # Long stable prefix at the top: system prompt, schemas, examples, RAG context.\
    SYSTEM = open("system_prompt.md").read()  # 8k tokens of stable instructions\
    SCHEMA = open("output_schema.json").read()  # 2k tokens of schema\
     \
    response = client.chat.completions.create(\
        model="gpt-5.4",\
        messages=[\
            {"role": "system", "content": SYSTEM + "\n\n" + SCHEMA},\
            # Variable user input at the bottom.\
            {"role": "user", "content": user_query},\
        ],\
    )\
     \
    # Inspect cache hits in the usage object.\
    print(response.usage.prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens)\
\
Two things to watch:\
\
1.  **Order matters.** Put stable content first, variable content last. If you concatenate user input into the middle of the system prompt, you bust the cache on every call.\
2.  **Minimum prefix length.** Caching kicks in above a minimum prefix size (currently 1024 tokens at the time of writing, verify on the live docs). Below that, no discount.\
\
Stretching credits: the batch API\
---------------------------------\
\
The batch API is the second lever, and it stacks with caching. The deal: you submit a JSONL file of requests, OpenAI processes them within 24 hours, and you pay 50 percent of the standard rate for every model.\
\
When to use it:\
\
*   Eval runs over a dataset of thousands of examples.\
*   Backfill jobs (classify all historical support tickets, re-embed a documentation corpus, etc.).\
*   Daily report generation that does not need to be live.\
*   Synthetic data generation for fine-tuning.\
\
When not to use it:\
\
*   Anything user-facing. The 24-hour window means you cannot stream a response back to a chat UI.\
*   Anything with a tight deadline. Batches often complete faster than 24 hours, but there is no SLA shorter than that.\
\
Submission looks like this:\
\
    from openai import OpenAI\
    client = OpenAI()\
     \
    # 1. Upload your JSONL file of requests.\
    batch_file = client.files.create(\
        file=open("requests.jsonl", "rb"),\
        purpose="batch",\
    )\
     \
    # 2. Submit the batch.\
    batch = client.batches.create(\
        input_file_id=batch_file.id,\
        endpoint="/v1/chat/completions",\
        completion_window="24h",\
    )\
     \
    # 3. Poll for completion, then download results.\
    print(batch.id, batch.status)\
\
Combine batch + caching on a stable-prefix workload and you can hit roughly 25 percent of list price. That is a 4x cost reduction for the exact same model output.\
\
Other ways to stretch credits\
-----------------------------\
\
A few smaller levers, in order of impact:\
\
*   **Pick the right model.** GPT-5.4-mini is materially cheaper than GPT-5.4 and handles a large fraction of production traffic with no measurable quality loss. GPT-5.4-nano is cheaper still for classification and simple extraction. Verify cost per task on your own evals, not vibes. See [How to Evaluate an LLM](https://www.respan.ai/articles/how-to-evaluate-an-llm)\
    .\
*   **Trim system prompts.** A 5,000-token system prompt that could be 1,500 tokens is paying 3.3x more per call, every call. Audit yours quarterly.\
*   **Use a gateway with semantic cache for FAQ-style queries.** Exact-match cache hits return in single-digit milliseconds at zero cost. For repeated user queries (support, docs, common tools), this can absorb 20 to 40 percent of traffic before it hits OpenAI.\
*   **Provider fallback for free-on-failure.** If OpenAI rate-limits you, falling back to Azure OpenAI or another provider keeps the request flowing without retry costs. See our [LLM Gateway pillar](https://www.respan.ai/ai-gateway)\
    .\
\
Common gotchas\
--------------\
\
1.  **Runaway agents.** An agent loop that fails to terminate (bad stop condition, retry on the wrong error) can burn $1,000 in an afternoon. Always set a per-run token budget and a hard monthly cap.\
2.  **Forgetting credits expire.** Unused prepaid credits expire 12 months after purchase. Do not over-stock.\
3.  **Credits applied to the wrong organization.** Promo codes are not transferable. Check the org switcher before redeeming.\
4.  **No auto-recharge in production.** If you forget to top up, your API returns 429 with `insufficient_quota` and your product breaks. Auto-recharge is not optional for production.\
5.  **Treating Usage dashboard as ground truth.** It lags by an hour or so. For real-time, log your own usage rows per request via a gateway or middleware.\
6.  **Mixing dev and prod on one org.** A noisy eval run from dev can blow through your prod budget. Use separate orgs (each with its own balance) for dev, staging, and prod.\
\
FAQ\
---\
\
**Do OpenAI API credits expire?** Yes. Prepaid credits expire 12 months after purchase. Promo and grant credits have their own expiration shown in the billing UI, usually 3 to 12 months.\
\
**Can I get a refund on unused credits?** Generally no for promo credits. For prepaid credits, OpenAI has historically only refunded under specific circumstances (account closure, dispute). Treat credits as non-refundable when you load them.\
\
**What happens when my balance hits zero?** The API returns 429 with an `insufficient_quota` error code. New requests fail immediately. Existing streaming responses already in flight complete. Top up to resume.\
\
**Is ChatGPT Plus the same as API credits?** No. ChatGPT Plus ($20/month) is a consumer subscription for the chat.openai.com web product. It does not grant any API credits. The API is billed separately and prepaid.\
\
**Can I use a single set of credits across organizations?** No. Each OpenAI organization has its own credit balance and billing. There is no transfer.\
\
**How do I cap spend at a hard dollar amount?** Set a "Monthly budget" in your organization's limits page. When usage hits it, the API returns 429 until next month. Combine with a "Usage alert" at 50 or 80 percent of budget for early warning.\
\
**Why is my bill higher than my Usage dashboard shows?** The Usage dashboard lags by roughly an hour. Reconcile with the Invoices page for billed totals. Also check: do you have other endpoints (embeddings, audio, image) you are forgetting?\
\

web.markdown:
[Skip to main content](https://blog.laozhang.ai/en/posts/chatgpt-plus-free-trial#main-content)

![ChatGPT Plus Free Trial 2026: No Public Trial, but 3 Official Ways to Get Plus Free](https://blog.laozhang.ai/posts/en/chatgpt-plus-free-trial/img/cover.webp)

ChatGPT Plus does not currently offer a public free trial that anyone can click and start on demand. As of March 29, 2026, OpenAI sells Plus as a $20 per month consumer plan and reserves free Plus access for a small set of exceptions: campaign-based invite links, the Australia and Colombia student referral program, and a free year for eligible U.S. servicemembers and veterans. If none of those apply to you, the honest try-before-you-buy path is ChatGPT Free first, then Go or paid Plus depending on how heavily you use the product.

Verification note: this article was rechecked against OpenAI pricing, help-center, and blog pages on March 29, 2026. Any offer that is not clearly backed by a current OpenAI page is treated here as stale or unverified rather than counted as a live method.

TL;DR
-----

If your question is simply "Does ChatGPT Plus have a free trial right now?", the practical answer is no for most readers. There is no public Plus button that says "start free trial" on the current consumer pricing flow. What exists instead is a patchwork of limited official routes with different eligibility rules, durations, and risks of misunderstanding.

Here is the current status map:

| Route | Current status on March 29, 2026 | Who it is for | Main catch |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Standard ChatGPT Plus plan | No public default trial | Everyone | Plus is sold as a paid $20/month plan |
| OpenAI promo or referral invite | Working, but campaign-dependent | Invited new users or users on Free | Not all accounts or regions get invites; some promos require a payment method |
| Australia / Colombia student referral program | Working, but narrow | Students at listed eligible schools | One month only, experimental, school-limited |
| U.S. servicemember / veteran offer | Working if eligible | Eligible U.S. servicemembers and veterans within 12 months of separation or retirement | Verification required; not a general public route |
| US / Canada student help page | Use caution | Eligible students in the US and Canada | OpenAI still hosts the help page, but the public claim flow is currently unclear |

The important shift in 2026 is that "ChatGPT Plus free trial" is no longer best understood as one product feature. It is an exception map. That means the fastest way to save time is to check whether you clearly fall into one of the documented buckets. If you do not, stop hunting for hidden workarounds and decide between ChatGPT Free, ChatGPT Go, and ChatGPT Plus based on your real usage.

ChatGPT Plus free trial status in 2026
--------------------------------------

OpenAI's own public pages are much clearer about what Plus is than many roundup articles are. The current Plus help page describes ChatGPT Plus as a $20 per month subscription, and OpenAI's January 16, 2026 announcement for ChatGPT Go describes the current global consumer lineup as Go at $8, Plus at $20, and Pro at $200. None of those pages describe Plus as having a standing public trial for everyone. The current consumer pricing flow also distinguishes between plans in a way that matters here: Plus shows a normal purchase path, while Business is the plan that carries a visible "Try for free" message. That distinction matters because rushed readers often see the free-trial language on the pricing page and assume it applies to Plus when it does not.

This is the first thing most articles get wrong. They start from the user's desire for free access and then inflate the method count by bundling together every adjacent thing they can find: old student promotions, expired partner bundles, unrelated Business trials, temporary mobile experiments, and plain ChatGPT Free access. That creates an article that looks comprehensive but is actually hard to trust. A more useful framing is much narrower: there is no default public Plus trial, but OpenAI does still run a few official free-access exceptions. If you qualify for one, great. If you do not, you should not waste an hour trying to reverse-engineer a 2025 campaign that may no longer be live.

That narrower framing is also better for decision-making. A reader searching this topic is usually not writing a market report. They are deciding whether to wait, sign up, or move on. The article therefore needs to answer two questions in sequence: first, is there a real public Plus trial? Second, if not, what is the safest next move? Everything else is supporting detail.

Official ways to get ChatGPT Plus free right now
------------------------------------------------

![Comparison board of the official documented routes to get ChatGPT Plus free in 2026](https://blog.laozhang.ai/posts/en/chatgpt-plus-free-trial/img/official-routes.webp)

### Promotional invites from OpenAI

This is the most straightforward official free-trial route that still exists for ordinary consumer accounts, but it is also the one that most readers cannot control. OpenAI's promotional-subscriptions FAQ says eligible users may receive a limited set of invite links or referral codes that grant a free, limited-duration trial of an otherwise paid ChatGPT subscription. The same article gives "for example, for 3 months" as one possible campaign shape. That means the route is real, but it is not universal and it is not one fixed offer.

Two practical details matter here. First, eligibility is campaign-dependent. OpenAI explicitly says not all users and not all regions are included in every promotion. Second, some promotions require a valid payment method to start the trial, and the same FAQ says the subscription can auto-renew into paid Plus unless you cancel before the promotional term ends. In other words, this is a real official path, but it is not a loophole and it is not guaranteed. If you receive an OpenAI email or in-product invite, treat it as a legitimate route worth using. If you do not, do not assume there is a hidden settings page where the offer is waiting for you.

### Australia and Colombia student referrals

OpenAI currently documents a student Plus referral program for Australia and Colombia. The help-center article, updated recently, says students from eligible schools in those countries can visit specific referral landing pages to get one month of ChatGPT Plus for free per new user sign-up. It also describes the program as experimental and explicitly limited to select countries and schools. That makes it a strong example of the broader 2026 pattern: official, useful, but narrow.

For students in those countries, this is the cleanest student route in the current documentation because the article still reads like a live access path rather than a legacy campaign archive. But even here, the key word is eligible. If your school is not on the list, or if you are outside those countries, this is not a general-purpose trial method that you can force to work by switching regions. It is a country-specific promotional program, not a global product feature.

If you are a student but this route does not fit your situation, the more useful next read is our guide to [ChatGPT student discounts and cheaper student options](https://blog.laozhang.ai/en/posts/chatgpt-student-discount)
, which covers what to do when the narrow official student promos do not apply.

### Free year for eligible U.S. servicemembers and veterans

This is the most generous official free-access route OpenAI currently documents, even though it is not really a "trial" in the ordinary sense. OpenAI announced a free year of ChatGPT Plus for eligible U.S. servicemembers and veterans who are within 12 months of retirement or separation. For readers who qualify, this is better than any short-term trial because it solves the access question completely for a year rather than letting you sample Plus for a week or a month.

The catch is obvious: eligibility is narrow, and verification is required. This is not a route for the general public, and it should not be padded into a generic "10 ways to get Plus free" article as if it were something anyone can activate. But it does belong in a current official map because it is both real and materially useful for the people it is meant for.

The US and Canada student page needs careful reading
----------------------------------------------------

![Side-by-side visual showing why the US and Canada student offer needs verification before relying on it](https://blog.laozhang.ai/posts/en/chatgpt-plus-free-trial/img/student-check.webp)

This is the one part of the current OpenAI documentation that deserves real caution instead of a confident yes or no. OpenAI still hosts a help-center article for "Student discounts for ChatGPT Plus (US/Canada)." The page says the offer is for students in the United States and Canada, describes SheerID verification, and even explains how two free months of Plus should be applied. If you read only that page, the natural conclusion is that the offer is live.

But the same help article also says the offer is valid for students who claim access from March 31, 2025 through May 31, 2025. And when I checked the public `chatgpt.com/students` route on March 29, 2026, it resolved to a generic students use-cases page rather than an obvious public claim flow. Those two facts do not fit together cleanly. The most defensible interpretation is not "the offer definitely still works" or "the offer is definitely dead." It is that the public documentation is internally inconsistent, so you should not treat this as a guaranteed 2026 path unless you actually see a live claim flow on your account or receive confirmation from OpenAI support.

That matters because student offers are the easiest kind of stale content to recycle. A page can stay live in a help center long after the headline campaign window has passed, especially if support still needs the article for people who are resolving old redemptions. If you are a US or Canadian student and you do see a live claim flow, use the official route and follow the instructions carefully. But if you are only seeing that older help article quoted on blogs, do not make a purchase decision based on the assumption that the two free months are broadly claimable right now.

What is expired, ambiguous, or not really a Plus trial
------------------------------------------------------

The easiest way to get misled on this topic is to treat every adjacent "free" signal as if it were a current Plus trial. That is how most bloated roundup articles get built. They are not always lying outright, but they are usually mixing categories that should stay separate.

The first category error is Business. OpenAI's pricing page shows free-trial language for Business, not for consumer Plus. That does not help an individual reader who just wants to test Plus. It is a different plan with a different use case and a different purchase flow. If a guide uses that Business trial language to imply that everyone can start Plus for free, it is flattening a distinction that OpenAI itself keeps clear.

The second problem is old partner-bundle claims. Guides from late 2025 often mentioned regional partnerships such as Rappi, Grab, or Shopee. I could not verify currently active official OpenAI claim pages for those routes on March 29, 2026 through official-domain searches. That does not prove every historic partnership has ended forever, but it does mean you should treat those articles as stale until you can find a live official claim page tied to the current campaign. In practice, that makes them poor candidates for a 2026 "working methods" list.

The third problem is vague mobile-trial folklore. Some community posts claim that app installs or dormant accounts can trigger Plus trial offers. Could isolated experiments exist? Possibly. But I did not find a current official OpenAI help article that documents a standing public mobile-app-only Plus trial for the general public. That means it does not belong in the main list of official routes. If you happen to see an in-app offer on your own account, evaluate it on its own terms. But it should not be presented as a reliable general method.

Finally, anything that requires buying third-party codes, joining shared accounts, or handing your login to a reseller is outside the scope of an official Plus free-trial guide. Even if one of those methods works in the short term, it is not the same thing as an OpenAI-documented free-access route.

If no trial applies, what should you do?
----------------------------------------

![Decision board showing when to use ChatGPT Free, Go, or Plus after ruling out a trial route](https://blog.laozhang.ai/posts/en/chatgpt-plus-free-trial/img/decision-flow.webp)

For most readers, this is the section that actually matters. Once you accept that there is no default public Plus trial, the question becomes: what is the least wasteful next step?

The simplest answer is ChatGPT Free first. OpenAI's free-tier FAQ makes clear that Free users can still do a lot: web search, file uploads, image creation, GPT discovery, and regular conversations with lower limits. If your real goal is just to feel the product and learn whether ChatGPT fits your workflow, Free is already the official try-before-you-buy option. It is not Plus, but it is the safest test drive because there is no billing setup, no hidden renewal, and no guesswork about eligibility.

The second option is ChatGPT Go. OpenAI's January 16, 2026 announcement positioned Go as the cheaper paid tier below Plus, priced at $8 per month in the US with localized pricing in some markets. For readers who mainly want more room than Free but do not yet need the full Plus toolset, Go is often the more rational stepping stone than endlessly hunting for a Plus trial that may never appear. This is especially true if your usage is regular but not intense.

Plus still makes sense when your work actually needs the upgrade. If you rely on ChatGPT for research-heavy workflows, long writing sessions, coding, or the higher-end tools and limits that paid tiers unlock, then paying $20 may be more sensible than spending hours chasing a narrow promo. If that is your situation, our detailed comparison of [Free vs Go vs Plus vs Pro](https://blog.laozhang.ai/en/posts/chatgpt-plus-worth-it-plans-comparison)
 is the better decision guide, and our breakdown of [ChatGPT Plus usage limits](https://blog.laozhang.ai/en/posts/chatgpt-plus-usage-limits)
 shows what you are really buying.

In short: if you qualify for one of the official exceptions, use it. If you do not, Free is the cleanest test, Go is the sensible lower-cost paid step, and Plus is worth paying for only when your workload actually needs it.

FAQ
---

**Do ChatGPT Plus promo invites require a credit card or payment method?**

Sometimes, yes. OpenAI's invite FAQ says some promotions require adding a valid payment method to start the trial. That is why you should read the redemption page carefully rather than assuming every official invite works the same way.

**Will a ChatGPT Plus promo auto-renew when the trial ends?**

Often, yes. OpenAI's invite FAQ says the subscription can auto-renew into the paid plan with the payment method on file unless you cancel before the promotional period ends. If you start an invite-based trial, set a calendar reminder immediately instead of relying on memory.

**Can existing Plus users use a student offer or invite promo?**

Usually not, or at least not automatically. Invite eligibility is campaign-specific, and the student documentation itself contains separate rules for different account states. In practice, assume that most promos are designed for new or Free-plan users unless the official campaign page explicitly says otherwise.

**Are old partner bundles like Rappi or Grab still a safe bet in 2026?**

Not without a current official claim page. Those offers were heavily cited in late 2025, but I could not verify currently active official OpenAI landing pages for them on March 29, 2026. Treat them as stale until you can prove otherwise from an official source.

**Is ChatGPT Free enough to test whether Plus is worth paying for?**

For many people, yes. If your real goal is to understand whether ChatGPT fits your daily workflow, the Free tier is already the cleanest first step. Upgrade only after you know the limits are actually slowing you down.
